characters.name: Alva Gunderson
characters.description: Alva GundersonAliasesGordy StewsonRelativesSky Gunderson(brother; deceased)AffiliationSteamlandHellMaruCitizenshipSteamlandBiographical informationStatusPresumed deadPhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderMale ♂Hair colorBrownProduction informationPlayed byRichard AyoadeFirst episode"Steamland Confidential"Latest episode"Goodbye Bean" Alva Gundersonis a citizen ofSteamlandandSky Gunderson's brother. He is also the founder of Steamland and head of Gunderson Steamwork company. He was the first to discover the potential of steam engine and is the head of the biggest company in the town, which makes him practically the ruler of Steamland. While he has some connections toSatan(the steam that powers Steamland comes fromHell) andQueen Dagmar(he suppliedMaruwith technology), he mostly acts as a seperate villain who follows his own agenda rather than conspire with Dagmar. His plans all involve getting his hands on Bean's magic and ultimately making her his bride. At first sight he seems like a nice and polite man but Bean still remained distrustful of him due to her experience with his brother Skybert. It is implied that Alva may not be as nice as he seems since even the Arch-Druidess warned Bean not to trust him.

characters.name: Arch-Druidess
characters.description: Arch-DruidessAliasesNew Mommy (Only Derek)ProfessionLeader of the ChurchAffiliationDreamlandSeekersSteamlandBiographical informationStatusPresumed deadPhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderFemale ♀Production informationPlayed byTress MacNeille  TheArch-Druidessis a recurring character inDisenchantment. She speaks with confidence and authority, even though the religion is still in its early stages. She even admits to being skeptical over the existence of theGodthat her religion worships.

characters.name: Bad Bean
characters.description: Bad BeanFirst appearanceFinal outfitBiographical informationAge19 (presumably)StatusDeceasedPhysical descriptionRaceDream-Time DoppelgängerHeight5'4Hair colorWhite Bad BeanisBean's evil alter-ego. Inititally she lived in Bean's head and encouraged her to do bad things. She is also partially responsible for Bean's nightmares. However, in "Bean Falls Apart", she managed to seperate herself from Bean and gained life as her own being. Bad Bean is incapable of love. She is mean toElfo,Luci,Miri, andBunty, and is also very manipulative. Unlike regular Bean, she admires Queen Dagmar and happily conspires with her to torment others and complete Dagmar's ultimate plan to unleash the magic of Dreamland.
characters.personality_section: Bad Bean is incapable of love. She is mean toElfo,Luci,Miri, andBunty, and is also very manipulative. characters.name: Barko
characters.description: BarkoProfessionPulling Pops wagonAffiliationElfwoodPhysical descriptionRaceDogGenderMale ♂Hair colorBlackProduction informationFirst episode"The Good, The Bad, and the Bum-Bum" Barkois a Scottish Terrier, who belonged toPopsback when Pops was still a travelling salesman for theElfwood Candy Company. Barko pulled Pops wagon with candy. When Pops first metQueen Grogda, she completely ignored him, but gave Barko a bone from the wild boar she had just killed. After Grogda and Pops eloped together and went to live in a treehouse, Barko also lived with them there. Barko was however left behind in the woods when the treehouse was burned down byGnomes. In the present, whenBean,JerryandLucicome to pick up Elfo withThe Compensator, they see a whole pack of Scottish Terriers, implying that Barko found himself a mate and had a family after he was abandoned.

characters.name: Bean
characters.description: BeanAliasesBeanTiabeaniePrincess TiabeanieBeanie (byKing ZøgandBunty)Ma'am (byBunty)Sister Tiabeanie (byMother Superior)Juanita Demonheimer (demon alias[1]TiaQueen Tiabeanie The First (title)Crazy Girl (byQueen Oona)Queen Tiabeanie Mariabeanie de la Rochambeaux GrunkwitzSibling Majesty (by Jasper)[2]RelativesKing Agøgg (paternal ancestor)King Trøg (paternal ancestor)King Wøg (paternal great-grandfather)King Xøg(paternal grandfather; deceased)Queen Bee-Baw(paternal grandmother; deceased)King Yøg(paternal uncle; deceased)King Zøg(father)Mariabeanie(maternal great-grandmother; deceased)Unnamed maternal grandparentsRebecca the Enchantress(maternal aunt)Emperor Cloyd(maternal uncle)Jerry(maternal uncle)Queen Dagmar(mother)King of Dankmire(former step-grandfather)Queen of Dankmire(former step-grandmother)Princess Uuuma of Dankmire(former step-relative)Queen Oona(former step-mother)Prince Derek(younger paternal half-brother)Jasper(younger paternal half-brother)Satan(step-father)Luci(older step-brother)Guci(older step-brother)Unnamed father-in-lawQueen Mermonia(mother-in-law)Rhonda(sister-in-law)Unnamed sisters-in-lawMora(wife)ProfessionQueen ofDreamland(abdicated)AffiliationDreamlandBiographical informationMarital statusPreviously Engaged 3 timesMarried toMoraAge19 (Beginning of series)StatusAlivePhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderFemale ♀Height5'4Hair colorWhiteProduction informationPlayed byAbbi JacobsonFirst episode"A Princess, an Elf, and a Demon Walk Into a Bar"Latest episode"Goodbye Bean" Not a traditional princess, Bean is quite the opposite, actively rebelling against her father,Zøg, who has driven her to drink. She is frustrated by the limited life choices available to her as a princess who is second in line to the throne. She resents the stereotypical role of a trophy queen, which is to have heirs and forge alliances in a loveless union, and refuses to marry the eligible princes presented to her (GuysbertandMerkimer), which would force her to inhabit this role. That said, at the beginning of the series she does act like an ungrateful brat who complains about how terrible her life is when she can course all she likes. Over time, Bean changes and becomes more mature and responsible, and by the end of Part 3 takes her role as a princess seriously. Once she is crowned Queen, Bean is eager to do a good job at it as shown when she worried about her kingdom's economy rather than the luxuries of being Queen and went to great lengths to protect her kingdom and Elfo when the Ogres' invade Dreamland to kidnap him.

Over time, Bean changes and becomes more mature and responsible, and by the end of Part 3 takes her role as a princess seriously. Once she is crowned Queen, Bean is eager to do a good job at it as shown when she worried about her kingdom's economy rather than the luxuries of being Queen and went to great lengths to protect her kingdom and Elfo when the Ogres' invade Dreamland to kidnap him.

characters.name: Bloaty the Squirrel
characters.description: Bloaty The SquirrelElfo's "Pet" SuirrelAffiliationDreamlandBiographical informationDate of birthDeceasedPhysical descriptionRaceSquirrelHair colorGrey Bloaty The Squirrelis a joke "character" inDisenchantment.It is made up byElfoas part of a ruse byBeanto try to getSvenand the vikings the drink the filthy, contaminated water from the water fountain. Although not exactly a pivotal character, and clearly not Elfo's pet, it does lead Elfo to address the curious naming conventions which take place in the show. (Elfo theelf, Sven the viking, Bloaty thebloatedsquirrel etc). By addressing a technique used by the show, Elfobreaks the fourth wall. (This is a post-modern technique). In so doing hehangs alampshadeon the unlikely act of Sven drinking such obviously filthy water. This is a different technique designed to show that the writer recognises this as an unlikely turn of events which, by addressing it and moving along, the audience's willing suspension of disbelief is maintained.

characters.name: Bob
characters.description: Bobwas agiantman that went on a rampage in the '80s. He may be one of the reasons people stereotype giants as being rampaging monsters with no thoughts, feelings, or rights.

characters.name: Bunty
characters.description: BuntyAliasesBuntsmçPrincess Tiabunty (ByBean)RelativesStan(Husband)Charlie (Son; Deceased)Allison(Daughter; Deceased)Bunty Junior(Presumed Daughter)Several Unnamed ChildrenAffiliationDreamlandBiographical informationMarital statusMarriedStatusAlivePhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderFemale ♀Hair colorBrownProduction informationPlayed byLucy MontgomeryFirst episode"A Princess, an Elf, and a Demon Walk Into a Bar"Latest episode"Goodbye Bean"  BuntyisBean's handmaid. In the absence of hermother, Bunty served as a maternal figure for Bean for most of her life. Bunty is a plump little lady, who is perfectly happy all the time, even when she's being mistreated. Bunty is a nice woman but also very dimwitted. She is very kind, with strong maternal instincts and always willing to help Bean and doing things for her such as watching over Bean while she has a hangover, playing along with her schemes when she tries to sleep with a Viking or letting her, Elfo and Luci stay at her home when King Zog kicked her out. Despite almost always maintaining this unnaturally cheerful demeanor, Bunty has shown on very rare occasions to secretly have an unspeakable amount of rage, bottled up inside of her, fueled by her mistreatment at her horrible job, her piss-poor salary, and her dying family.

characters.description: Bunty Junioris a daughter ofBuntywho looks like a miniature version of her mother, and even wears the same outfit. She was conceived inBean's bed.

characters.name: Emperor Cloyd
characters.description: Emperor CloydHumanPuppetRelativesMariabeanie(grandmother; deceased)Unnamed parents (deceased)King Zøg(former brother-in-law)Queen Dagmar(older sister)Queen Bean(niece)Becky the Enchantress(older sister)Jerry(younger brother)Satan(brother-in-law)Luci(step-nephew)Guci(step-nephew)ProfessionEmperor ofMaruAffiliationMaruBiographical informationMarital statusUnknownDate of birthUnknownStatusDeceased (as of "The Stience of Homemade Lightning")Physical descriptionRaceHumanGenderMale ♂Production informationPlayed byRich Fulcher Emperor Cloydis a recurring antagonist inDisenchantment.He is the uncharismatic ruler of the Empire ofMaruwhich is served by his older sister,Becky the Enchantress. He is also the younger brother ofDagmar, the older brother ofJerry, and the maternal uncle of thePrincess Bean. Both he and Becky are killed by Dagmar and Bad Bean at the of the series after Dagmar feels that they are no longer useful for her anymore. Cloyd's clothing looks like magick ceremonial attire. He wears a purple robe with a mixture of layers, a mushroom symbol with an eye (a recurring symbol) on a band around his neck, and a masonic apron (the shape that looks like "M" might be for "Maru". He also wears thick-rimmed glasses.

He also wears thick-rimmed glasses.

characters.name: Crones
characters.description: TheCronesare a group of elderly ladies who live inMaru. They are being led byPrudence. The Crones maintain the last section of the old Maru that is still untainted by the damage caused to the land by theMaru Royal House. It is implied that they are old enough to have known the Maru from before the current royal family took over. At least one of them also admitted to having been responsible for cursingBean's family. They are most prominently featured in "The Stience of Homemade Lightning", in which they train Bean to use her magic to defeatEmperor CloydandBecky the Enchantress, thus saving Maru and restoring the land to it's old state.

They are most prominently featured in "The Stience of Homemade Lightning", in which they train Bean to use her magic to defeatEmperor CloydandBecky the Enchantress, thus saving Maru and restoring the land to it's old state.

characters.name: Crown-wearing deer
characters.description: Crown-wearing deerCitizenshipEnchanted forestPhysical descriptionRaceDeerGenderMale ♂Hair colorBrownProduction informationLatest episode"Goodbye Bean" This unnamedDeeris a minor character. The deer lives in theEnchanted Forest. He first appears when King Zøg decides to go hunting alone, armed with a crossbow, to take his mind off Dagmar's betrayal. While stalking the deer, he stumbles and his crown falls from his head, before the deer picks it up with one of its antlers. Much later, when Zøg returns to the woods to try to find Ursula the sylvan selkie, retracing the route he took just before meeting her, he stumbles upon the same deer, loses his crown again and the deer, still wearing the first crown he picked up, takes the second, which enrages Zøg once again. In "Goodbye Bean",King Zøggave hisown crownto the Deer after settling in the woods withUrsulaandJasper.

characters.name: Dankmirian Hillbillies
characters.description: TheDankmirian Hillbilliesare a duo of farmers who live deep in the swamps ofDankmire. They kidnappedKing Zøg,Prince Derek,Princess Bean,Elfo, andLuciwith the intention of cooking and feeding them to a hungrySwamp Monster, but only to be devoured by the creature themselves.

characters.name: Darrell
characters.description: Darrellis the lone employee at theGift Shoppe at the Edge of the WorldinDisenchantment. He is a member of the Secret Society. He appears to be either pre-pubescent, or in puberty, with an annoying voice.

characters.name: Prince Derek
characters.description: Prince DerekAliasesKing Derek the First (byOdval)Bonnie Prince Derek (by himself)RelativesKing of Dankmire(maternal grandfather)Queen of Dankmire(maternal grandmother)Princess Uuuma of Dankmire(relative)Queen Oona(mother)King Agøgg (paternal ancestor)King Trøg (paternal ancestor)King Wøg (paternal great grandfather)King Xøg(paternal grandfather; deceased)Queen Bee-Baw(paternal grandmother; deceased)King Yøg(paternal uncle; deceased)King Zøg(father)Queen Bean(older paternal half-sister)Mora(sister-in-law)Jasper(younger paternal half-brother)Snarla(girlfriend)ProfessionPrince of DreamlandKing of Dreamland (briefly)Pirate PrinceAffiliationDreamlandBiographical informationMarital statusIn a relationshipDate of birthAliveAge14StatusSet sail with Oona and make his career as a pirate prince.Physical descriptionRace50%Salamander50%HumanGenderMale ♂Hair colorBlackProduction informationPlayed byTress MacNeilleFirst episode"A Princess, an Elf, and a Demon Walk Into a Bar"Latest episode"Goodbye Bean"  "Dad, look at the size of that skillet." Derek is shy, quiet, naive and introverted. He is shown to love to play with his toys, and loves to watch the news. Derek is afraid of skillets due to being almost cooked alive. He has a tendency to do things that embarrasses his father but to a lesser extent than his half-sister such as walking into his parents' bedroom when the both of them are naked and having a private conversation on their bed in episode 6 of season 1. In "Love's Slimy Embrace", he admitted that he did not even want to become King. He gained more confidence after being trained bySagathaand her fairy friends.

characters.name: Dr. Mulberry
characters.description: Dr. MulberryProfessionHead DoctorAffiliationTwinkle Town Insane AsylumCitizenshipTwinkletownBiographical informationAgeUnknownStatusAlivePhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderMale ♂Hair colorDark BrownProduction informationFirst episode"The Cabinet of Dr. Chazzzzz"Latest episode"The Cabinet of Dr. Chazzzzz" Dr. Mulberryis a dubious psychiatrist in theTwinkletown Insane Asylum. Despite his doctorate, he seems to be very confused, which causes great discomfort among his patients.

characters.name: Dragon Eggs
characters.description: Dragon Eggsare 'easter eggs' which refer specifically to the HBO seriesGame of Thrones. Although creatorMatt Groeningwas keen to dispel rumours thatDisenchantmentisbasedon the popular fantasy epic, he did acknowledge thatsome references were allowed into the show.[1]Given the popularity ofG.O.T, it was arguably impossible for other referencesnotto have snuck in. This page is an attempt to document their occurrence as well as provide some analysis of their narrative role. Whilst speculative in nature, this page takes as it's inspiration what is known to have been intentionally introduced (canon). Building on those and noticing other connections - which may be more subtle and less "canonical"- is interesting and illuminating. Her silver hair, which she inherited from her mother,Queen Dagmar, is similar to that of Daenerys Targaryen, and is the most obvious canonical reference. This type of hair, as well as very pale complexion, is a familial characteristic of theHouse Targaryen.

characters.name: Dreamland's soldiers
characters.description: Dreamland's soldiersmake up the military army of the kingdom ofDreamland. Among their ranks they have footsoldiers, archers and knights. Their main weapons are bows & arrows, swords, and other medieval weapons like halberds. Their uniforms for most of the series consist of full body armor and helmet, sometimes with a blue or blue/red tunic. However, afterQueen DagmarandSatantake over Dreamland in season 3, all the soldiers are given new uniforms consisting of black armor with spikes on the shoulders and helmets. They also haveGoonsadded to the army.

characters.description: Duke of TwinkletownProfessionNoblemanAffiliationTwinkletownPhysical descriptionRaceHumanGenderMale ♂Hair colorBrownProduction informationPlayed byMaurice LaMarcheFirst episode"Love's Slimy Embrace" TheDuke of Twinkletownis a nobleman fromTwinkletownwho is first seen in "Love's Slimy Embrace". The duke is a short, obese man with brown hair, a small moustache and a tanned skin.
